title: 20 screening in Australia Official news
wikitext:
[]: 2.0 screening in Australia (Official news!)             Quote Post by KaaVI » Tue Apr 27, 2010 10:37 pm Madman entertainment australia distributes 97% of Australia's and New Zealand's anime on DVD. Every year (with an exception to last year) they host a Reel Anime festival that showcases the most popular and recent anime films in Japan. I am here to announce that Evangelion 2.0 will be playing as part of this festival as announced in Madman's Anime Update magazine (2010 May/June issue pg. 12-13) Just as a reference i scanned the magazine pages which reveal this awesome news.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Apr 28, 2010 4:08 am And just like Madman there is no real information on when it will be. Woo.

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Wed Apr 28, 2010 4:13 am Fazmotron wrote: And just like Madman there is no real information on when it will be. Woo.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Apr 28, 2010 4:32 am Well, its been a year since they said they were going to screen it. September last year's issue to be precise. Then they said, "oh wait, no Reel Anime this year, too bad".

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Apr 28, 2010 5:14 am That's basically the god tier double feature right there. Hell yeah Summer Wars . Superior in every feasible way to 2.0 IMO. However the plot twists and turns in 2.0 will definitely set one up for the amazing satisfaction that is Summer Wars . Fun Fact: Sadamoto was in charge for both of the character designs in those films.

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Wed Apr 28, 2010 5:29 am ran1 wrote: That's basically the god tier double feature right there. Hell yeah Summer Wars . Superior in every feasible way to 2.0 IMO. However the plot twists and turns in 2.0 will definitely set one up for the amazing satisfaction that is Summer Wars . Fun Fact: Sadamoto was in charge for both of the character designs in those films.

[]:             Quote Post by ryatwo » Wed Apr 28, 2010 5:47 am I know beggars can't be choosers, but I want 2.22.......... But really, it's about time we got a screening of 2.0. Hey KaaVI, wanna come with?

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Wed Apr 28, 2010 5:51 am ryatwo wrote: Hey KaaVI, wanna come with?

[]:             Quote Post by rilocay » Sat May 01, 2010 5:36 am This, is awesome news. Will be there first screening!

[]:             Quote Post by KaaVI » Sun May 02, 2010 8:30 am rilocay wrote: Will be there first screening!

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sun May 02, 2010 10:24 am The previous Reel Anime screenings have been all across Australia; Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide, and Canberra. So its most likely they will screen in the same places again.

[]:             Quote Post by rilocay » Mon May 03, 2010 6:52 am KaaVI wrote: Looks at your info, You live in Melbourne so i hope you can afford to come all the way to Sydney to watch it

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Mon May 03, 2010 8:58 pm When I saw the summer wars trailer the first thing I thought of was the first Digimon Movie, that kind of beautiful animation

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon May 03, 2010 11:34 pm wonderluster wrote: When I saw the summer wars trailer the first thing I thought of was the first Digimon Movie, that kind of beautiful animation

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue May 04, 2010 3:23 am Well, same director for the roughly a third/half of the English Digimon Movie. The English Digimon Movie is three Japanese movies. But yes. The similarities are especially blatant in the cyberspace scenes.

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Tue May 04, 2010 7:11 am It's blatant in plot, too. "Hey, a huge object is locked onto our location and going to kill us if we don't destroy the virus in less than 10 seconds!" Missile, satellite, tomato, tomahto. Oh yes, and having millions of random people join together for a final strike. Omnimon? Oh no, it's super Sailor Transformation Natsu!

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Jul 02, 2010 5:10 am Well well well, looks like we get some news: [url]http://www.madman.com.au/news/real-anime-2010-%E2%80%93-theatrical-season-announced/[/url] [url]http://www.madman.com.au/reelanime/[/url] Also, blog post: [url]http://www.evageeks.org/2010/07/reelanime-2010-evangelion-2-0-australian-screening/[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Touka » Sun Jul 04, 2010 12:23 pm If Redline is at the festival I highly recommend checking that out as well. It is phenomenal. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XhwZL1Osh_U

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Thu Jul 08, 2010 5:45 am Fazmotron wrote: The previous Reel Anime screenings have been all across Australia; Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide, and Canberra . So its most likely they will screen in the same places again.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Thu Jul 15, 2010 1:35 am The cinema locations for ReelAnime 2010 Australia and New Zealand have been announced! Reel Anime 2010 Sceening Details  SPOILER: Show
